methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.5B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.75B

private.name: Lambda
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.5B+

data.narrative:
Ayar Labs, a Series E AI & Software company, is evaluated against a peer set of 3 same-stage private companies in the sector (Crusoe Energy at $10.0B, SandboxAQ at $5.8B, Lambda at $2.5B), alongside broader public comparables including Palantir (PLTR). The company has raised $870M+ to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $3.8B.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression anchors to the $3.8B last round and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement, yielding $5.2B. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the three same-sector, same-stage comparables, which have a median valuation of $5.8B and a range of $2.5B to $10.0B, directly implying $5.8B. The two methods produce a blended triangulated range of $4.5B (low) to $6.6B (high), with a median of $5.5B.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $4.5B to $6.6B, with $5.5B as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include dispersion within the peer set—a 4x spread from Lambda to Crusoe Energy—and limited visibility into the specific revenue and growth profiles differentiating Ayar Labs from comparables. The gap between last post-money ($3.8B) and the triangulated median ($5.5B) reflects anticipated Series E momentum, though execution risk remains material at this stage.
